events and functions.\n- There are no signs of obfuscation, malicious code, or suspicious logic.\n- The code does not process untrusted data beyond accepting standard parameters (address, signer/provider).\n\n---\n\n### Confirmed Issues:\n- **Presence of code:** The code matches the description of a standard ERC20 factory, with no anomalies or malicious behavior.\n- **Malware:** The malware score is 0 across all reports, consistent with the absence of malicious intent.\n- **Obfuscation:** All reports agree on no obfuscation (score 0).\n- **Security risk:** Very low (around 0.1), primarily because it facilitates interaction with arbitrary token contracts, which is standard behavior.\n\n### Logical Flaws or Mistakes:\n- No logical errors or suspicious patterns are identified.\n- The reports correctly interpret the code as boilerplate for contract interaction, with no hidden malicious intent.\n\n### Scores:\n- The malware scores are all 0, which is appropriate.\n- The obfuscated scores are 0, correct.\n- The security risk scores are low (~0.1), reasonable given the context.\n\n### Potential Concerns:\n- The only potential \"risk\" is if a user connects to a malicious or compromised token contract, but this is inherent to interacting with any arbitrary contract and not specific to this code.\n\n---\n\n### Critical Evaluation:\n- The reports are consistent and accurate.\n- They correctly identify that this is autogenerated, standard code with no malicious or obfuscated content.\n- The security risk is minimal and appropriate.\n\n---\n\n### Final Justification:\n- Since all reports agree, and the code is a standard, boilerplate factory for ERC20 tokens, I would assign the following scores:\n  - **Malware:** 0 (no malicious behavior detected)\n  - **Obfuscated:** 0 (no obfuscation)\n  - **Risk:** 0.1 (very low, standard interaction code)\n\n### Additional Notes:\n- The only \"risk\" here is typical of any contract interaction code—connecting to untrusted contracts—but this is not a flaw in the code itself.\n- No evidence of backdoors, data exfiltration, or malicious logic.\n\n---\n\n### **Summary of my
